method: Numeral-run cardinality / compound-quantity structure. A numeral-run = maximal run of consecutive num tokens in an inscription stream; p_compound = fraction of runs with length>=2. NULL = token-order shuffle WITHIN each inscription (preserves type multiset), 1000 draws, two-sided p. Positive control: plant clustering (excess) and spreading (deficit) on the pooled LA type multiset; false-positive rate on shuffled true-H0 streams (20 sets). Cross-site on sites with >=20 numeral-runs; leave-one-site-out on Haghia Triada. Pure token-count structure (L2/L3); no numeral-value interpretation, no arithmetic, tokens anonymous.
key_findings: Every numeral-run in the LA corpus is length 1: 1276/1276 runs are single num tokens; p_compound = 0.0 exactly. There are ZERO adjacent num-num pairs anywhere in the corpus.

Under the within-inscription token-order shuffle null, the expected p_compound is 0.181 (numerals would cluster into compound runs ~18% of the time by chance). Observed 0.0 is a massive and significant DEFICIT (p=0.0, two-sided), not excess: numerals are spread out far more than chance, not clustered.

The deficit is perfectly consistent across all 8 testable sites (>=20 runs): Haghia Triada, Zakros, Khania, Phaistos, Arkhalkhori, Tylissos, Malia, Palaikastro all show p_compound=0.0 with significant deficit (p<=0.05); zero sites show excess.
Leave-one-site-out (excluding Haghia Triada) preserves the effect: p_compound=0.0, null mean 0.152, p=0.0, deficit. The structure is not HT-driven.
Positive control PASSED: planted clustering detected as excess (p=0.0) and planted spreading detected as deficit (p=0.0); false-positive rate 0/20=0.0 on shuffled true-H0 streams (<=0.10 required).
successor_hypotheses: E045: Test whether the non-num token that SEPARATES consecutive numerals is structurally constrained — is it always a word (logogram) immediately before and a line-break (nl) immediately after, i.e. a rigid word+quantity+newline ledger cell? Quantify the prev/next token-type distribution around num tokens vs shuffle null.

E046: If quantities are single tokens, test whether the numeral VALUE (still not interpreted arithmetically — only its raw magnitude as stored) correlates with the preceding word token, to ask whether single-numeral quantities attach to specific commodity words (a lexical-attachment structure test, L2/L3, no phonetics).

E047: Compare numeral-run cardinality between LA and a Linear B DAMOS stream if a comparable token stream can be derived; ask whether LB (known to use integer+fraction compounds) shows compound runs where LA does not — a cross-script structural divergence at L2/L3.

E048: Test for POSITIONAL regularity of num tokens within the line: are numerals always line-final (last token of a line)? Build a line-segmented stream and measure the positional distribution of num tokens vs a within-line shuffle null.

E049: Examine whether the rare inscriptions with multiple num tokens on one line place them in a fixed order relative to intervening word tokens (e.g. word-num-word-num) — a within-line quantity-slot structure test, L2/L3, anonymous tokens.

E050: Power-up the deficit test with an analytic (exact) null for p_compound under random placement of k num tokens among n stream slots, to confirm the shuffle-null deficit without Monte Carlo and to derive a closed-form effect size.
non_circular: Pure token-count structure (L2/L3). No phonetics, no sound, no meaning, no reading assigned. Numeral VALUES are NOT interpreted and NO arithmetic is performed — only the COUNT of consecutive num tokens (run length) is measured. Tokens are ANONYMOUS. The positive control is built on the LA type multiset itself (LB lacks a comparable stream-level numeral-run structure in the available data) with a token-order-shuffle null; this is stated explicitly and does not use the LA result to validate itself. The shuffle null preserves each inscription type multiset and only permutes order, so it cannot be circular with the observed run structure.
deviations: LB positive control was not available: the LB DAMOS data accessible to this worker does not expose a stream-level token sequence with num tokens, so the positive control was built on the LA pooled type multiset with synthetic clustering/spreading injection plus a token-order-shuffle false-positive check, as explicitly permitted by the protocol. This is a control on the LA stream itself, not a cross-script LB benchmark.
n_draws for the global/cross-site/LOO shuffle null = 1000 (>=1000 required). PC detect tests used n_draws=1000; false-positive check used 20 sets (>=20 required).
